An objective classification system use to document and track the development of breasts in female children during puberty.
Only the papilla is elevated above the level of the chest wall.
Elevation of the breasts and papillae may occur as small mounds along with some increased diameter of the areolae.
The breasts and areolae continue to enlarge, although they show no separation of contour.
The areolae and papillae elevate above the level of the breasts and form secondary mounds with further development of the overall breast tissue.
Mature female breasts have developed. The papillae may extend slightly above the contour of the breasts as the result of the recession of the areolae.

preferred_term: Tanner Genitalia Development Scale in Males
definition: An objective classification system use to document and track external genital development in male children during puberty.

synonyms: Tanner Genitalia Development Stage 1, Male|Tanner Genitalia Stage 1|Tanner Genitalia Stage 1, Male|Tanner stage 1
definition: The testes, scrotal sac, and penis have a size and proportion similar to those seen in early childhood.

synonyms: Tanner Genitalia Development Stage 2, Male|Tanner Genitalia Stage 2|Tanner Genitalia Stage 2, Male|Tanner stage 2
definition: There is enlargement of the scrotum and testes and a change in the texture of the scrotal skin. The scrotal skin may also be reddened, a finding not obvious when viewed on a black and white photograph.

synonyms: Tanner Genitalia Development Stage 3, Male|Tanner Genitalia Stage 3|Tanner Genitalia Stage 3, Male|Tanner stage 3
definition: Further growth of the penis has occurred, initially in length, although with some increase in circumference. There also is increased growth of the testes and scrotum.

synonyms: Tanner Genitalia Development Stage 4, Male|Tanner Genitalia Stage 4|Tanner Genitalia Stage 4, Male|Tanner stage 4
definition: The penis is significantly enlarged in length and circumference, with further development of the glans penis. The testes and scrotum continue to enlarge, and there is distinct darkening of the scrotal skin. This is difficult to evaluate on a black-and-white photograph.

synonyms: Tanner Genitalia Development Stage 5, Male|Tanner Genitalia Stage 5|Tanner Genitalia Stage 5, Male|Tanner stage 5
definition: The genitalia are adult with regard to size and shape.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Scale in Females
synonyms: Pubic Hair Development Scale, Females|Tanner Pubic Hair Development in Females|Tanner Pubic Hair Development, Females
definition: An objective classification system use to document and track the development of pubic hair in female children during puberty.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Scale in Males
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Scale, Males|Tanner Pubic Hair Development in Males|Tanner Pubic Hair Development, Males
definition: An objective classification system use to document and track the development of pubic hair in male children during puberty.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 1, Female
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Female
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 1|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 1|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 1, Female|Tanner stage 1
definition: Vellus hair develops over the pubes in a manner not greater than that over the anterior wall. There is no sexual hair.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 1, Male
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Male
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 1|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 1|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 1, Male|Tanner stage 1
definition: Vellus hair appears over the pubes with a degree of development similar to that over the abdominal wall. There is no androgen-sensitive pubic hair.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 2, Female
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Female
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 2|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 2|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 2, Female|Tanner stage 2
definition: Sparse, long, pigmented, downy hair, which is straight or only slightly curled, appears. These hairs are seen mainly along the labia. This stage is difficult to quantitate on black and white photographs, particularly when pictures are of fair-haired subjects.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 2, Male
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Male
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 2|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 2|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 2, Male|Tanner stage 2
definition: There is sparse development of long pigmented downy hair, which is only slightly curled or straight. The hair is seen chiefly at the base of penis. This stage may be difficult to evaluate on a photograph, especially if the subject has fair hair.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 3, Female
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Female
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 3|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 3|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 3, Female|Tanner stage 3
definition: Considerably darker, coarser, and curlier sexual hair appears. The hair has now spread sparsely over the junction of the pubes.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 3, Male
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Male
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 3|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 3|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 3, Male|Tanner stage 3
definition: The pubic hair is considerably darker, coarser, and curlier. The distribution is now spread over the junction of the pubes, and at this point that hair may be recognized easily on black and white photographs.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 4, Female
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Female
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 4|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage , Female|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 4|Tanner stage 4
definition: The hair distribution is adult in type but decreased in total quantity. There is no spread to the medial surface of the thighs.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 4, Male
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Male
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 4|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 4|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 4, Male|Tanner stage 4
definition: The hair distribution is now adult in type but still is considerably less that seen in adults. There is no spread to the medial surface of the thighs.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 5, Female
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Female
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 5|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 5|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 5, Female|Tanner stage 5
definition: Hair is adult in quantity and type and appears to have an inverse triangle of the classically feminine type. There is spread to the medial surface of the thighs but not above the base of the inverse triangle.

preferred_term: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 5, Male
parent_name: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage, Male
synonyms: Tanner Pubic Hair Development Stage 5|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 5|Tanner Pubic Hair Stage 5, Male|Tanner stage 5
definition: Hair distribution is adult in quantity and type and is described in the inverse triangle. There can be spread to the medial surface of the thighs.
An objective classification system use to document and track testicular development in male children during puberty.
Testicular volume < 4 mL or long axis < 2.5 cm.
Testicular volume 4-8 mL (or 2.5 to 3.3 cm long).
Testicular volume 9-12 mL (or 3.4 to 4.0 cm long).
Testicular volume 15-20 mL (or 4.1 to 4.5 cm long).
Testicular volume > 20 mL (or > 4.5 cm long).
